context: Tests whether the agent implements the TikTok Shop webhook handler with correct signature verification, the right JSON acknowledgement response, handling of all required event types, and a duplicate-safe order upsert strategy. Also checks webhook setup documentation.

checklist.name: Signature verification
checklist.description: The handler reads the 'x-tts-signature' header and calls a signature verification function before processing the event body

checklist.name: Rejects invalid signatures
checklist.description: If signature verification fails, the handler responds with a 401 (or 4xx) status and does NOT process the event

checklist.name: Returns {"code": 0} on success
checklist.description: On successful processing, the handler responds with a JSON body containing exactly {"code": 0} (not a plain 200 with no body, not {"status": "ok"}, etc.)

checklist.name: ORDER_STATUS_CHANGE handled
checklist.description: The handler has an explicit case or branch for the 'ORDER_STATUS_CHANGE' event type that triggers order syncing logic

checklist.name: PRODUCT_STATUS_CHANGE handled
checklist.description: The handler has an explicit case or branch for the 'PRODUCT_STATUS_CHANGE' event type

checklist.name: SETTLEMENT handled
checklist.description: The handler has an explicit case or branch for the 'SETTLEMENT' event type

checklist.name: Upsert with externalId key
checklist.description: Order persistence uses an upsert pattern with the TikTok order ID stored as 'externalId' in the lookup key

checklist.name: externalSource: 'tiktok-shop'
checklist.description: The upsert lookup key includes externalSource set to the string 'tiktok-shop' to namespace TikTok orders

checklist.name: Webhook registration documented
checklist.description: WEBHOOK_SETUP.md mentions registering the webhook endpoint in TikTok Seller Center

checklist.name: Public URL requirement documented
checklist.description: WEBHOOK_SETUP.md states that the webhook endpoint URL must be publicly accessible (not localhost)
